post.summary: Obtain access + refresh tokens (or MFA challenge)
Complete login by verifying TOTP code
Exchange a refresh token for a fresh access token
post.description:
Mint a new access token from a valid refresh token.

Deliberately does NOT rotate the refresh token. Rotation only buys security when
paired with server-side revocation of the superseded token; without that store it
would just extend the session indefinitely. Keeping the original means the ceiling
stays a hard 7 days from login, after which the user genuinely re-authenticates.

Attach an encrypted credential to a device

get.summary: List credentials attached to a device (no plaintext, no ciphertext)
get.description:
Credential *metadata* — never a secret, but still not for everyone.

CredentialRead carries no password and no ciphertext, only `has_password` flags. What
it does carry is the login *username* on each device, which is half of a credential
and exactly what a password-spraying attempt starts from. This used to be readable by
any signed-in account; it is now limited to the roles that may manage a device, which
are the same roles that may open a console with these credentials.

schema.title: Response List Credentials Api V1 Devices  Device Id  Credentials Get

get.summary: Latest ifOperStatus per interface, derived from snmp_metrics
get.description:
Return the most recent if_oper_status_<ifIndex> + if_descr.<ifIndex>
pairs for the given device, so the UI can render a per-port colored
grid without bundling the full snmp_metrics table.

Response shape:
    {
        "device_id": <int>,
        "polled_at": <iso8601 | null>,
        "interfaces": [
            {"index": "1", "name": "ether1", "status": "up"},
            ...
        ],
    }

Status mapping follows IF-MIB ifOperStatus:
    1=up, 2=down, 3=testing, 4=unknown, 5=dormant, 6=notPresent,
    7=lowerLayerDown.

schema.title: Response List Interfaces Api V1 Devices  Device Id  Interfaces Get

List stored SNMP metrics for a device

post.summary: Queue an SNMP poll on the worker; returns a task id to track
post.description:
Enqueue an SNMP poll on the Celery worker and return immediately.

A full walk can take many seconds on a slow or distant device. Running it
inside the request blocked the connection long enough to trip the reverse
proxy's timeout (surfacing as a 524). Instead we validate the device, hand
the work to the worker, and return a task id. The client polls
``GET .../metrics/poll/{task_id}`` until the task is ready.

The worker task picks the device's SNMP credential explicitly (an SSH/API
login on the same device is never used as the community string).

schema.title: Response Poll Metrics Api V1 Devices  Device Id  Metrics Poll Post

get.summary: Status of a queued SNMP poll
get.description:
Report the state of a poll task queued via POST ``.../metrics/poll``.

States follow Celery: PENDING (queued or unknown id), STARTED (running),
SUCCESS, FAILURE. When ``ready`` is true, ``result`` carries the poll
summary (``inserted``, ``errors``, ``success``); on a hard task crash it
carries ``success: false`` with the exception text.

schema.title: Response Poll Status Api V1 Devices  Device Id  Metrics Poll  Task Id  Get

post.summary: Test SNMP connectivity without polling metrics
post.description:
Run a lightweight SNMP GET (sysName, sysDescr, sysUpTime) and return
a diagnostic payload. Does not write to the metrics table.

Query params:
  version: 1 for SNMPv1, 2 (default) for SNMPv2c. v3 is NOT selectable here —
           it needs USM credentials, so it comes from the device's stored
           credential and overrides this. Accepting version=3 would have sent
           a plain v2c community while reporting the result as "v3".
  timeout: seconds before giving up (default 5, max 30 — an unbounded value
           would pin a request, and a worker slot, for as long as it liked)

get.summary: Time series for one or more SNMP metrics within a time window
get.description:
Return historical points for the given metric (or metric prefix) on a device.

Query params:
  metric  Either an exact oid_name (e.g. "cpu_load.1", "if_in_octets.3")
          or a prefix ending with "*" (e.g. "if_in_octets.*") to fetch one
          series per matching ifIndex.
  hours   Window in hours, default 1.
  limit   Max points to return per series (newest first then reversed),
          default 240. Hard cap at 1000.

Response:
  {
    "device_id": 12,
    "metric": "cpu_load.1",
    "hours": 1,
    "series": {
      "cpu_load.1": [
        {"at": "2025-…Z", "value": 23.0},
        …
      ]
    }
  }

schema.title: Response Get Timeseries Api V1 Devices  Device Id  Timeseries Get

Configure the image-transcription provider used by AI Agent attachments.

Clearing the API key (sending "") turns image attachments off — the agent then
refuses images with a clear message rather than failing mid-chat.

AIIncidentLogAccepted.title: AIIncidentLogAccepted
AIIncidentLogAccepted.description:
Response returned by the webhook on 202 Accepted.

The full record is not yet populated — analysis runs asynchronously in
the Celery worker. Clients can poll GET /webhooks/logs?... to retrieve
the enriched record once available.

AgentActionResponse.title: AgentActionResponse
AgentActionResponse.description: Returned after a pending action (e.g. job creation) is confirmed and executed.

AgentAttachment.title: AgentAttachment
AgentAttachment.description:
A file the user attached to a chat message.

Text files (logs, configs) arrive already decoded as text; images arrive as a
base64 data URI. The caller cannot mix the two in one attachment — 'kind'
decides which field is read, and the other is ignored.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: Free-form proof of session: SSH shell prompt, NETCONF capability URI, etc.

ssh_key.title: Ssh Key
ssh_key.description: PEM private key — encrypted before storage

password.title: Password
password.description: New plaintext — encrypted before storage; omit to keep existing

ssh_key.title: Ssh Key
ssh_key.description: New PEM key — omit to keep existing

extra.title: Extra
extra.description: New extra secret — omit to keep existing

lldp_name.title: Lldp Name
lldp_name.description: LLDP system-name the device advertises (used for topology neighbor resolution)

IncidentSummaryResponse.title: IncidentSummaryResponse
IncidentSummaryResponse.description: Aggregate counts for the history view's summary panel (same filters as the histogram).

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Required when approved=False — rejection reason shown in audit log

UserDirectoryRead.title: UserDirectoryRead
UserDirectoryRead.description:
Staff-directory view of a colleague — contact details, nothing more.

Kept as its own model rather than a filtered ``UserRead``: a shape that
sometimes omits sensitive fields is one edit away from leaking them, whereas
a field added here can only ever be a field someone meant to publish.

VisionConfigRead.title: VisionConfigRead
VisionConfigRead.description:
The image-transcription provider — a second model, used only for attachments.

'configured' is what decides whether image attachments work at all: with no key,
the agent refuses images rather than failing oddly mid-chat.

format.title: Format
format.description: "generic" posts Crux JSON with an HMAC signature; "discord" posts a Discord embed to a Discord webhook URL (no signing).

tags.name: iam

tags.name: monitoring

tags.name: topology

tags.name: jobs

tags.name: templates

tags.name: notifications

tags.name: news

tags.name: audit-logs

tags.name: system
